title: Auditory-Visual Shift Study
description: This dataset comprises scalp EEG recordings from 49 participants (19 younger adults aged 20-40 years and 30 older adults aged 49-73 years) performing an interleaved dual-oddball audio-visual attention task. The study investigates age-related differences in selective attention and sensory processing by examining event-related potentials during three task conditions: focus visual, focus auditory, and attention shift between modalities. Data were collected from 33 EEG channels plus EOG and mastoid reference channels at 250 Hz sampling rate.
methods_description: Scalp EEG data were acquired from 33 channels using 10-20 electrode placement, referenced to the right mastoid, with additional left mastoid and two EOG channels. Sampling rate was 250 Hz with an analog passband of 0.01-60 Hz. Input impedances were maintained below 5 kilo-ohms. Participants performed an interleaved dual-oddball task with randomly interleaved auditory (500/550 Hz tones) and visual (light/dark blue squares) stimuli presented at stimulus-onset asynchronies of 200-800 ms, with task cues (Look/Hear) interspersed at mean 5-second intervals.
